support.summary: Exec connect can be run by the deal owner's manager, not the CEO
support.detail: The 300k Rompetrol S2P deal lacks CPO/CEO engagement (0 buyer-side exec touchpoints in 8 months), but the recommended fix is an executive-connect call led by the deal owner's manager with the CPO to validate budget and positioning. That is a VP/SVP-level action, not a CEO-to-CEO peer relationship, and there is no blocked discount, roadmap commitment, or presales guarantee requiring the CEO personally. A VP/CRO can fully own this, so CEO support is not irreplaceable.
support.why_not_vp: The needed exec connect is a manager-to-CPO validation call the deal owner's own manager can lead; no CEO veto, pricing approval, or peer-CEO relationship is required.

monitor.reason: No qualifying signal within the last 14 days; only two generic July 1 procurement emails, no close-date push, no recent calls, and the outstanding items are buyer-dependent.
